name: A2ASDOCT
scale: AREDS2 ancillary SD-OCT cohort with 38,400 B-scans from 384 subjects; 269 intermediate AMD and 115 control subjects
typicalTasks: Public AMD-focused OCT cohort used for quantitative classification of eyes with and without intermediate AMD.

name: ACRIMA
scale: 705 fundus images
typicalTasks: Optic-disc-centered fundus benchmark with 396 glaucomatous and 309 normal images.
linkedArticles: [{"citationKey": "e5136d5c95b2", "slug": "e5136d5c95b2", "title": "Deep Learning Approaches for Retinal Disease Diagnosis: Insights from Fundus and OCT Analysis", "year": 2025}]

name: AI Challenger OCT dataset
area: Macular edema / retinal OCT lesion segmentation
scale: AI Challenger 2018 macular edema dataset with 83 OCT volumes and 10,880 OCT slices
typicalTasks: Used for macular-edema-related lesion segmentation, including subretinal fluid and pigment epithelial detachment.

name: AROI dataset
area: Retinal layers and fluid in neovascular AMD
scale: 1,136 annotated B-scans from 25 patients out of 3,200 B-scans
typicalTasks: Public annotated retinal OCT image database. Used for joint retinal layer, lesion, and fluid segmentation in healthy and neovascular AMD eyes.
linkedArticles: [{"citationKey": "40e5cd2458a7", "slug": "40e5cd2458a7", "title": "Structural-prior guided and feature-enhanced transformer with masked image modeling pretraining for retinal layers and fluid segmentation in macular edema OCT images", "year": 2025}, {"citationKey": "c3ee99b25fe5", "slug": "c3ee99b25fe5", "title": "HyFormer: a hybrid transformer-CNN architecture for retinal OCT image segmentation", "year": 2024}]

name: DRAC 2022 / OCTA-DR
scale: 1,103 UW-OCTA images
typicalTasks: Consolidates DRAC 2022, Diabetic Retinopathy Analysis Challenge 2022, DRAC OCTA, and OCTA-DR where used for the same DR challenge context. Tasks include DR lesion segmentation, image-quality assessment, and DR grading.
linkedArticles: [{"citationKey": "1a93f0883cd2", "slug": "1a93f0883cd2", "title": "CSANet: a lightweight channel and spatial attention neural network for grading diabetic retinopathy with optical coherence tomography angiography", "year": 2024}, {"citationKey": "8ba2213f7ff2", "slug": "8ba2213f7ff2", "title": "A Vision Transformer Based Deep Learning Architecture for Automatic Diagnosis of Diabetic Retinopathy in Optical Coherence Tomography Angiography", "year": 2023}]

name: Duke / DUKE SD-OCT / Duke DME OCT dataset
area: Diabetic macular edema; retinal layer and fluid analysis
scale: 110 annotated B-scans from 10 DME patients
typicalTasks: Consolidates Duke dataset, DUKE, DUKE OCT dataset, Duke SD-OCT, Duke spectral domain OCT dataset, and Chiu-style Duke DME references. Used for retinal layer, boundary, and fluid segmentation.

name: EyePACS
scale: 35,126 labeled training images in the Kaggle DR release
typicalTasks: Fundus benchmark for diabetic retinopathy grading. Included where studies combine fundus and OCT / OCTA resources.

name: FAROS
scale: 40 OCTA images from 40 subjects
typicalTasks: Fully Annotated Retinal OCTA Segmentation dataset. Used for retinal microstructure segmentation and quantitative OCTA analysis.
linkedArticles: [{"citationKey": "b5f971387b12", "slug": "b5f971387b12", "title": "Deep learning-based segmentation and quantitative analysis of retinal microstructures in optical coherence tomography angiography images using RS_Unet3+", "year": 2026}]

typicalTasks: Consolidates UCSD, Kermany, Kaggle OCT, Mendeley OCT, OCT2017, OCT 2017, labeled OCT, publicly available validated OCT, and similar aliases. Used mainly for four-class retinal disease classification.

name: OCTDL
scale: 2,064 high-resolution OCT images from 821 patients; seven disease / condition groups
typicalTasks: Consolidates OCTDL and OCTDL dataset entries. Used for image-level retinal disease classification across AMD, DME, ERM, RAO, RVO, VID, and normal classes.
linkedArticles: [{"citationKey": "769ac8041ab3", "slug": "769ac8041ab3", "title": "Integrating lightweight convolutional neural network with entropy-informed channel attention and adaptive spatial attention for OCT-based retinal disease classification", "year": 2025}, {"citationKey": "96ce472e1aee", "slug": "96ce472e1aee", "title": "OCT Vision: Retinal Disease Detection Using Deep Learning", "year": 2024}, {"citationKey": "0d6a03135510", "slug": "0d6a03135510", "title": "Segmentation of Retinal Layers in OCT Images Using Deep Learning Algorithms for Medical Diagnostics", "year": 2024}]

name: OCTID
scale: 572 OCT images; five classes
typicalTasks: Consolidates OCTID benchmark / public entries. Used for classification of normal, AMD, CSR, macular hole, and diabetic retinopathy.
linkedArticles: [{"citationKey": "7abb94fc2719", "slug": "7abb94fc2719", "title": "Enhanced AMD detection in OCT images using GLCM texture features with Machine Learning and CNN methods", "year": 2025}, {"citationKey": "769ac8041ab3", "slug": "769ac8041ab3", "title": "Integrating lightweight convolutional neural network with entropy-informed channel attention and adaptive spatial attention for OCT-based retinal disease classification", "year": 2025}]

name: OCTMNIST
scale: 109,309 source OCT images standardized to a resolution of 28 × 28 pixel
typicalTasks: MedMNIST benchmark for four-class OCT classification derived from OCT2017 / Kermany-style OCT data. Kept separate from the clinical-resolution Kermany / UCSD dataset because MedMNIST provides standardized low-resolution benchmark images and predefined splits.
linkedArticles: [{"citationKey": "cf8018eec68f", "slug": "cf8018eec68f", "title": "A Hybrid CBAM-Vision Transformer for Enhanced Classification of Retinal Diseases in Optical Coherence Tomography Imagery", "year": 2025}]

name: OCTA-500 / OCTA-3M / OCTA-6M
modality: OCT and OCTA volumes, projections, labels
scale: 500 subjects with 3 mm × 3 mm and 6 mm × 6 mm fields of view
typicalTasks: Consolidates OCTA-500 benchmark / public entries and OCTA-3M / OCTA-6M aliases where used as OCTA-500 subsets. Used for vessel, FAZ, artery, vein, capillary, and retinal-layer segmentation.
linkedArticles: [{"citationKey": "1af22a193a23", "slug": "1af22a193a23", "title": "Retinal Disease Classification from Bimodal OCT and OCTA Using a CNN–ViT Hybrid Architecture", "year": 2025}, {"citationKey": "7f80043b52dd", "slug": "7f80043b52dd", "title": "OCT(2)Former: A retinal OCT-angiography vessel segmentation transformer", "year": 2023}, {"citationKey": "4491e0db91c1", "slug": "4491e0db91c1", "title": "VCT-NET: An Octa Retinal Vessel Segmentation Network Based on Convolution and Transformer", "year": 2022}]

name: OCTA-SS
scale: 55 parafoveal ROI slices from 3 mm × 3 mm OCTA images of 11 participants
typicalTasks: Used for OCTA vessel segmentation. Kept separate because it is referenced as a distinct OCTA benchmark and provides detailed superficial-layer vessel annotations.
linkedArticles: [{"citationKey": "7f80043b52dd", "slug": "7f80043b52dd", "title": "OCT(2)Former: A retinal OCT-angiography vessel segmentation transformer", "year": 2023}]

name: OPTIMA challenge
scale: Challenge retinal-fluid dataset commonly described as 30 OCT volumes
typicalTasks: Consolidates Optima and OPTIMA challenge. Used for intraretinal cystoid fluid segmentation in OCT. Kept separate from RETOUCH because the dataset names and challenge lineage differ.
linkedArticles: [{"citationKey": "7f53aa5cb1c9", "slug": "7f53aa5cb1c9", "title": "Deep learning architectures analysis for age-related macular degeneration segmentation on optical coherence tomography scans", "year": 2020}]

name: ORIGA
scale: 650 fundus images in ORIGA-light; commonly reported as 168 glaucoma and 482 normal images
typicalTasks: Fundus benchmark for optic disc / cup analysis and glaucoma detection. Included where paired or externally compared with OCT measurements.
linkedArticles: [{"citationKey": "8d687ff87530", "slug": "8d687ff87530", "title": "IoT based automatic detection of glaucoma disease in OCT and fundus images using deep learning techniques", "year": 2020}]

name: RetinalOCT-C8 / OCT-C8
scale: 24,000 OCT images across eight classes, balanced at 3,000 images per class
typicalTasks: Consolidates OCT-C8, OCT-C8 dataset, and RetinalOCT-C8. Used for eight-class retinal OCT disease classification across AMD, CNV, CSR, DME, DR, drusen, macular hole, and normal.

name: RETOUCH challenge
area: Retinal fluid; AMD and retinal vein occlusion
scale: 112 OCT volumes and 11,334 B-scans overall
typicalTasks: Consolidates RETOUCH, ReTOUCH, MICCAI RETOUCH challenge, RETOUCH challenge dataset, and RETOUCH retinal OCT fluid detection challenge. Used for IRF, SRF, and PED detection and segmentation.

name: ROSE-1
scale: 117 OCTA images from 39 subjects in ROSE-1
typicalTasks: Consolidates ROSE-1 and Retinal OCTA vessel Segmentation-1. Used for OCTA vessel segmentation.
linkedArticles: [{"citationKey": "b5f971387b12", "slug": "b5f971387b12", "title": "Deep learning-based segmentation and quantitative analysis of retinal microstructures in optical coherence tomography angiography images using RS_Unet3+", "year": 2026}, {"citationKey": "7f80043b52dd", "slug": "7f80043b52dd", "title": "OCT(2)Former: A retinal OCT-angiography vessel segmentation transformer", "year": 2023}]

name: SERI-CUHK SD-OCT
scale: SERI dataset reported as 32 SD-OCT volumes; CUHK dataset reported as 43 SD-OCT volumes
typicalTasks: Used for diabetes-related retinal disease classification, including healthy, DME, and DR-DME categories in source studies. Kept separate from A2A because the names indicate distinct source datasets.
